AccountType.description: DebitAccountType corresponds to the JSON schema field "debit_account_type".

description.description: Human-readable description of the accounting treatment

credit_account_code.description: Account code in decimal form (1-5 digits)

currency_scale.description: Currency scale from ledger (e.g., 100 for cents, 1 for whole units)

debit_account_code.description: Account code in decimal form (1-5 digits)

event_id.description: Event ID that originated this transfer

is_rollup.description: Whether this is a rollup transfer

leg_number.description: Template leg that generated this transfer

Event.description: Optional test event to validate against template

$schema.description: Identifier for the schema used to construct this event

accounts.description:
Account mappings for the event. Each account must have a unique purpose value.
May be empty when all accounts are template-defined (ConstantAccounts).

amount.description: Transaction amount in minor currency units (cents, pence, satoshis)

directives.description: Processing directives (unique). Replaces legacy reserved keys in extra_metadata.

event_id.description: UUID v7 for idempotency and natural time ordering. This is the idempotency key — on failure, always retry with the same event_id. Never generate a new event_id for a retry.

event_source.description: System or service that originated the event

ledger_timestamp.description: Optional event-level ledger timestamp used for seeding/backfill; distinct from occurred_at

original_event_id.description: First event in the series (optional linkage for analytics)

previous_event_id.description: Immediate predecessor event in the chain (optional linkage for analytics)

site_id.description: SiteID corresponds to the JSON schema field "site_id".

template_ids.description: Array of template IDs to process atomically (ALL must succeed or ALL fail)

account_id.description: UUID of the account (with cached string form)

EventEcho.description: Minimal event echo for the validated event.

EventValidationTrace.description: Optional execution trace when explain mode is requested

transfers.description: Transfers that WOULD be generated if event were processed

event_id.description: EventID corresponds to the JSON schema field "event_id".

processed_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds (UTC) when event processing was initiated.

EventsPostResponseStatus.description: Acknowledgement status at response time. POST /events always returns COMMITTED.

leg_number.description: Template leg number that produced this transfer

ExtraMetadata.description: Additional context validated against template's metadata_schema

FinancialImpact.description: Net impact on this account type

id.description:
Clean policy ID (no URI scheme)
Examples: "IFRS9.B5.4.1", "IAS32.DEPOSITS", "INT/FEES-001"

version.description: Optional explicit version (if nil, resolve active version)

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

errors.description: Optional structured error details for field-level validation failures.

instance.description:
A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.
This service uses the request path.

status.description: The HTTP status code generated by the origin server.

title.description:
A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.
Use the HTTP status text (e.g., "Bad Request").

type.description:
A URI reference that identifies the problem type.
The service uses service-scoped problem type URIs.

checks_list.description:
ChecksList provides a stable, human-friendly ordering of checks for UIs.
It mirrors the entries in Checks but in a consistent order (by name).

failed_templates.description: Templates that failed to load with errors

Template.description: Template corresponds to the JSON schema field "template".

$schema.description: Descriptor identifying the JSON schema that defines this template payload

account_code_validations.description: Validations for account codes by purpose

accounting_treatments.description: Accounting treatment classifications and policy references

capabilities.description: Capabilities toggle optional behaviours and overrides for this template.

description.description: Detailed description of template purpose and behavior

event_type.description: Type of event this template processes

examples.description: Example scenarios for testing and documentation

financial_statements_impact.description: Net accounting impact on financial statements by account type

legs.description: Transfer legs that generate the actual ledger transfers

site_id.description: SiteID corresponds to the JSON schema field "site_id".

template_id.description: TemplateID corresponds to the JSON schema field "template_id".

trigger.description: Business event that triggers this template

validations.description: Business rule validations that must all pass for the event to be processed

variables.description:
Variables for calculations and business logic. Evaluated sequentially, can
reference previous variables.

description.description: Human-readable description of the account type

mask.description: Fixed-width digit pattern for valid account codes. Bare "*" matches any code.

description.description: Human-readable description of the example scenario

expected_transfers.description: Expected transfers that should be generated for validation

description.description: Human-readable description explaining the business context of this impact

ledger_scope.description:
Ledger scope identifier. Must be lower_snake_case and match an entry in ledger_scope_descriptions.
@pattern ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:_[a-z0-9]+)*$

TemplateLedgerScopeDescriptions.description: Human-readable descriptions for each ledger scope used by this template

amount.description: Reference for the transfer amount (variable or direct selector)

condition.description: Optional reference to a boolean controlling conditional execution

credit_account.description: Reference for the credit account (variable or direct selector)

debit_account.description: Reference for the debit account (variable or direct selector)

description.description: Human-readable description of this leg's purpose

ledger_scope.description:
Ledger scope identifier. Must be lower_snake_case and match an entry in ledger_scope_descriptions.
@pattern ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:_[a-z0-9]+)*$

leg_count.description: Number of legs in the template

TemplateMetadataSchema.description: JSON Schema for validating event's extra_metadata field

$schema.description: Schema corresponds to the JSON schema field "$schema".

additionalProperties.description:
AdditionalProperties corresponds to the JSON schema field
"additionalProperties".

properties.description: Properties corresponds to the JSON schema field "properties".

required.description: Required corresponds to the JSON schema field "required".

type.description: Type corresponds to the JSON schema field "type".

description.description: Human-readable description of what this validation ensures

expression.description: CEL expression that must evaluate to true

expected_transfers.description: Optional expected transfers for comparison (uses sparse ExpectedTransfer format)

auto_generated.description: True if expected_transfers were auto-generated from the event_request

comparison_errors.description: Differences between expected and actual transfers

generated_expected_transfers.description: Generated transfers in sparse expected-transfer format if event_request was provided

validation_details.description: Structured validation error details with enhanced information

validation_errors.description: List of validation errors if any, provided in simple string form

description.description: Human-readable description of what this variable represents

name.description: Variable name used in legs and other expressions

value.description: CEL expression to calculate the variable value

event_id.description: Domain-specific metadata (mapped to TigerBeetle's UserData fields)

id.description: Transfer ID (populated on reads from TigerBeetle, zero value on writes)

TreatmentType.description: Accounting treatment type for this leg - must match one of the template's accounting_treatments

field_path.description: Field path where the error occurred (e.g., "variables[0].expression", "legs[2].amount")

invalid_value.description: The problematic value that caused the error (if applicable)

suggestion.description: Suggested fix or action to resolve the error

ValidationErrorLocation.description: Line/column information for CEL expressions (if applicable)

templateCacheRefreshRequestBody.description: Request body for template cache refresh operations.

info.description:
# Event Service API

## Start here

The Event Service processes financial events into accounting transfers using configurable templates and CEL expressions.

This OpenAPI document is generated from Go source annotations and is served by the running service at `GET /openapi.json`.

## Quickstart

1. Run the service locally:

   - `just run`

2. Open the API docs portal:

   - `GET /docs`

3. Sanity check liveness/readiness:

   - `curl -s http://localhost:8080/livez | jq .`
   - `curl -s http://localhost:8080/readyz | jq .`

4. Submit an event:

   - `POST /events` with a `domain.Event` payload.

### Example: `POST /events`

Request (schema-valid example):

```bash
curl -sS -X POST http://localhost:8080/events \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "$schema": "https://ledger-rocket.github.io/schemas/domain/event.schema.json",
    "event_id": "0192b4f0-7abc-7def-8000-000000000001",
    "site_id": 1,
    "occurred_at": 1760000000000,
    "template_ids": [532],
    "amount": 100000,
    "event_source": "docs_example",
    "accounts": [
      {"account_id": "0192b4f0-7abc-7def-8000-000000000001", "purpose": "from_account"},
      {"account_id": "0192b4f0-7abc-7def-8000-000000000002", "purpose": "nostro_account"}
    ],
    "directives": ["skip_rollup_processing"]
  }'
```

Response (example):

```json
{
  "status": "COMMITTED",
  "event_id": "0192b4f0-7abc-7def-8000-000000000001",
  "processed_at": 1760000000123
}
```

### Example: `GET /events/{event_id}/transfers`

Request:

```bash
curl -sS 'http://localhost:8080/events/0192b4f0-7abc-7def-8000-000000000001/transfers?limit=200' | jq .
```

Response (example):

```json
{
  "transfers": [
    {
      "id": "0192b4f0-7abc-7def-8000-000000000010",
      "debit_account_id": "0192b4f0-7abc-7def-8000-000000000001",
      "credit_account_id": "0192b4f0-7abc-7def-8000-000000000002",
      "amount": 100000,
      "template_id": 532,
      "ledger_id": 1,
      "event_id": "0192b4f0-7abc-7def-8000-000000000001",
      "occurred_at": 1760000000000,
      "leg_index": 1,
      "is_rollup": false,
      "timestamp": 1760000000123,
      "flags": 0
    }
  ],
  "count": 1,
  "has_more": false
}
```

## Core concepts (from the implemented API + functional specification)

### Event IDs and idempotency

- `event_id` is a UUIDv7 and is used as the idempotency key for `POST /events`.
- Submitting the same `event_id` more than once returns `409 DUPLICATE_EVENT`.

### Acknowledgements and the outbox

- `POST /events` returns `api.EventsPostResponse`.
- `status` is always `COMMITTED` for `POST /events`.
- `COMMITTED` means TigerBeetle succeeded and a durable outbox record exists, so the event is recoverable even if downstream publication is still pending.
- Publication to the downstream Event Store is asynchronous; clients should use transfer lookups as the durable source of truth.

### Validation endpoints

- `POST /events:validate` runs the validation pipeline without committing transfers.
- `POST /events:validate:explain` returns the same response shape and additionally includes a deterministic execution trace (`trace`).
- `POST /templates:validate` validates a template and can optionally compare generated vs expected transfers.

### Transfer lookups and enrichment

- Transfer lookup endpoints read from the configured Transfer Ledger (TigerBeetle when enabled).
- “Enriched” transfer variants require `site_id` to resolve account context.

## API surface (inventory)

### Events

- `POST /events` — process an event and return an acknowledgement
- `GET /events/{event_id}` — fetch the original event payload (when supported by the configured Event Store)
- `GET /events/{event_id}/outbox` — fetch outbox state/timestamps for the event
- `GET /events?original_event_id={original_event_id}` — list events sharing `original_event_id` (when supported by the configured Event Store)

### Validation

- `POST /events:validate` — dry-run event validation
- `POST /events:validate:explain` — dry-run event validation with trace
- `POST /templates:validate` — validate a template (optionally with event + expected transfers)

### Transfers

- `GET /transfers/{id}` — fetch a transfer by ID
- `GET /transfers/{id}/enriched` — fetch an enriched transfer by ID (requires `site_id`)
- `GET /events/{event_id}/transfers` — list transfers for an event
- `GET /events/{event_id}/transfers/enriched` — list enriched transfers for an event (requires `site_id`)
- `GET /accounts/{account_id}/transfers` — list transfers for an account
- `GET /accounts/{account_id}/transfers/enriched` — not implemented (documented as `501 NOT_IMPLEMENTED`)

### Templates

- `GET /sites/{site_id}/templates` — list templates for a site
- `GET /sites/{site_id}/templates/{template_id}` — fetch a single template
- `PUT /sites/{site_id}/templates/{template_id}` — create/update template draft content
- `POST /sites/{site_id}/templates/{template_id}/submit` — submit template for review
- `POST /sites/{site_id}/templates/{template_id}/approve` — approve template
- `POST /sites/{site_id}/templates/{template_id}/reject` — reject template
- `POST /sites/{site_id}/templates/{template_id}/withdraw` — withdraw template (undo submit)
- `POST /sites/{site_id}/templates/{template_id}/deprecate` — deprecate template
- `POST /sites/{site_id}/templates/{template_id}/delete` — delete draft template
- `POST /sites/{site_id}/templates/{template_id}/undelete` — restore deleted draft
- `GET /sites/{site_id}/templates/{template_id}/audit-slugs` — list template audit slugs
- `GET /sites/{site_id}/templates:status` — template loader status for a site
- `POST /sites/{site_id}/templates:refresh-cache` — refresh template cache for a site

### Health

- `GET /` — service information
- `GET /livez` — liveness
- `GET /readyz` — readiness

## Schemas

Primary request/response schemas (non-exhaustive):

- `domain.Event` — request body for `POST /events`, `POST /events:validate`, `POST /events:validate:explain`
- `api.EventsPostResponse` — acknowledgement returned by `POST /events`
- `api.EventValidationsPostResponse` — response returned by event validation endpoints
- `api.TemplateValidationsPostRequest` / `api.TemplateValidationsPostResponse` — request/response for template validation
- `api.TransfersListResponse` / `api.EnrichedTransfersListResponse` — list responses for transfer lookups
- `ProblemDetails` — RFC 9457 Problem Details (standard error response for all 4xx/5xx)

## Error handling

All 4xx/5xx responses use RFC 9457 Problem Details:

- Media type: `application/problem+json; charset=utf-8`
- Body fields (and only these): `type`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, `instance`, optional `errors`
- `type` is a service-scoped problem type URI under `https://ledger-rocket.dev/event-service/problems/...`
- `detail` is explicit and actionable when possible (e.g., missing `account_id` values with `site_id`, or required capabilities for cross-ledger validation).

## Auth

Authentication is optional and controlled by configuration:

- When `auth.require_oauth: true`, the service enforces OAuth/JWT validation and scopes (see `docs/auth-config.md`).
- The OpenAPI document does not yet declare `securitySchemes`.

## Versioning and compatibility

This is not specified in the provided material:

- The HTTP paths are not versioned (no `/v1/...` prefix).
- A compatibility/deprecation policy is not defined.

## TODOs / gaps to close

1. Define and document the authentication model (and declare it in OpenAPI `securitySchemes`).
2. Define and document the public base URL(s) and any environment-specific routing (reverse proxy / root path expectations).
3. Define a versioning strategy and deprecation/changelog policy for the HTTP API.

## Launch-ready checklist

1. Ensure every operation has a precise description, complete parameters, and explicit error responses in OpenAPI.
2. Add 1–2 schema-valid request/response examples for each non-debug endpoint.
3. Add tag descriptions and groupings suitable for a public portal (ReDoc/Scalar).
4. Declare auth + required headers (API key / OAuth / mTLS) once the platform decision is final.
info.title: Event Service API

get.description: Returns basic service information including service name, version, and health endpoints.
Retrieves all transfers where the specified account is either the debit or credit account.

parameters.description: Filter by transfer side: 'debit' or 'credit'. Omit for both sides.
parameters.name: side

parameters.description: Maximum number of transfers to return (server default: 200)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Upper bound (inclusive) for transfer timestamp in Unix milliseconds
parameters.name: timestamp_max

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Counts transfers where the specified account is either the debit or credit account using a single capped TigerBeetle query.

parameters.description: Filter by transfer side: 'debit' or 'credit'. Omit for both sides.
parameters.name: side

parameters.description: Upper bound (inclusive) for transfer timestamp in Unix milliseconds
parameters.name: timestamp_max

get.description: Returns outbox record metadata, optionally filtered by state.

parameters.description: Filter by state (PREPARED, COMMITTED, PUBLISHED, FAILED)
parameters.name: state

get.description: Returns aggregate outbox stats including counts, ages, and retry info.
Returns events that share the same original_event_id with cursor-based pagination. Ordered by linked_at ASC, event_id ASC.

parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return (server default: 200)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response
parameters.name: cursor

post.description: Processes an event through the pipeline, durably prepares the outbox, commits transfers to the configured Transfer Ledger, and returns a COMMITTED acknowledgement once the event is durably recoverable.

get.description: Returns the original event payload exactly as submitted when retrieval is supported by the configured Event Store.
Returns the current outbox record if the configured outbox still retains it.
Retrieves transfers associated with an event by querying the Transfer Ledger for transfers whose `user_data_128` matches the event ID.

parameters.description: Maximum number of transfers to return (server default: 200)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Upper bound (inclusive) for transfer timestamp in Unix milliseconds
parameters.name: timestamp_max

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Retrieves all transfers for an event with account metadata. Requires site_id to resolve account context.

parameters.description: Maximum number of transfers to return (server default: 200)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Upper bound (inclusive) for transfer timestamp in Unix milliseconds
parameters.name: timestamp_max

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response
parameters.name: cursor

get.summary: Get enriched transfers by event ID

get.description: Counts transfers associated with an event using a single capped TigerBeetle query.

parameters.description: Upper bound (inclusive) for transfer timestamp in Unix milliseconds
parameters.name: timestamp_max

post.description: Validates an event without submitting to TigerBeetle or storing. Runs full validation pipeline including template compilation, account enrichment, and business rule checks.
Validates an event and returns detailed execution trace showing variable evaluations, validation outcomes, and leg resolution. Used for debugging and troubleshooting template logic.
post.summary: Validate event with execution trace (explain mode)

get.description: Retrieves all templates configured for a given site
get.summary: List all templates for a site

get.description: Retrieves a specific template for a given site
get.summary: Get a single template by ID

post.description: Reloads templates for a site from the configured store. If template_ids is omitted, reloads all templates for the site.

schema.description: Optional: reload only specified template IDs

requestBody.description: Optional: reload only specified template IDs

get.description: Returns template cache status for a site.

post.description: Validates a template's structure, CEL expressions, and business logic. Optionally runs against a provided event to generate and/or compare expected transfers.

get.description: Searches transfers by template ID and/or ledger ID. At least one filter must be provided.

parameters.description: Maximum number of transfers to return (server default: 200)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Upper bound (inclusive) for transfer timestamp in Unix milliseconds
parameters.name: timestamp_max

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response
parameters.name: cursor

Retrieves a specific transfer with account metadata. Requires site_id to resolve account context.

get.description: Counts transfers by template ID and/or ledger ID using a single capped TigerBeetle query. At least one filter must be provided.

parameters.description: Upper bound (inclusive) for transfer timestamp in Unix milliseconds
parameters.name: timestamp_max

get.summary: Count transfers by template and/or ledger

get.description: Simple health check that only fails if service is shutting down. Used by load balancers to determine if service should receive traffic.
Checks all dependencies (TigerBeetle, Ledger Service, Template Store). Fails if ANY dependency is unhealthy. Used to determine if service is ready to handle requests.
